name: Regen Commons
tagline: Urban commons for regenerative economics and civic technology
description: Regen Commons is an Amsterdam-based hub bridging civic technology, regenerative economics and community governance. It operates as a co-working and event space for DAOs, cooperatives, and grassroots civic initiatives, with a strong focus on financial sustainability through innovative economic models.

spaces.name: Commons Floor
spaces.notes: Open-plan co-working area with hot desks and dedicated team corners

spaces.name: Assembly Room
spaces.notes: Modular event space for meetups, assemblies and workshops

spaces.name: Studio Zero
spaces.notes: Focused workspace for visiting researchers and resident fellows

accommodation.notes: Partnership with two nearby hostels and a co-living house for visiting residents. Subsidized rates available for residency participants.

assets.name: DAO Tooling Stack
assets.notes: Maintained suite of governance tools including Snapshot, Gnosis Safe and custom voting interfaces

assets.name: High-Speed Network
assets.notes: Dedicated 10Gbps fiber connection with VPN and self-hosted services

assets.name: Recording Studio
assets.notes: Podcast and video recording studio with professional lighting and sound

challenges.title: Design a credit system for community contributions
challenges.problem_description: Members contribute to the space in different ways — hosting events, mentoring, maintaining infrastructure — but there is no fair system to recognize and reward these contributions beyond informal acknowledgment.
challenges.expected_solution: A token-based credit system that tracks contributions, enables peer recognition, and can later be integrated with the broader Hubs Network platform.

challenges.title: Strengthen partnerships with local government
challenges.problem_description: Despite strong grassroots networks, the hub has limited connection with municipal policy-making processes. Amsterdam's civic innovation programs could be a major asset.
challenges.expected_solution: Formalized collaboration with Amsterdam Smart City initiative and participation in at least two municipal innovation programs per year.
